title: Wayland window with wgpu
notes: winit to open Wayland window, wgpu device + surface + render pipeline. Just clear to ThermalPalette::BG color.
prompt:
Create a new file crates/thermal-conductor/src/renderer.rs that implements WgpuState. It must:
1. Implement `WgpuState::new(window: &winit::window::Window) -> anyhow::Result<Self>` using `wgpu::Instance::new`, `instance.create_surface(&window)`, `instance.request_adapter`, `adapter.request_device`. Store `surface`, `device`, `queue`, `surface_config`.
2. Implement `WgpuState::render(&mut self) -> Result<(), wgpu::SurfaceError>` that calls `surface.get_current_texture()`, creates a `CommandEncoder`, begins a `RenderPass` with `load: wgpu::LoadOp::Clear(wgpu::Color { r:0.039, g:0.0, b:0.063, a:1.0 })` (that is ThermalPalette::BG = #0a0010), and submits.
3. Implement `WgpuState::resize(&mut self, new_size: winit::dpi::PhysicalSize<u32>)` that updates surface_config.

Update crates/thermal-conductor/src/main.rs to:
- Add `mod renderer;`
- Replace the current tmux smoke-test body with a winit EventLoop that: creates a window titled "THERMAL CONDUCTOR", initialises WgpuState, handles WindowEvent::RedrawRequested by calling renderer.render(), handles WindowEvent::Resized by calling renderer.resize(), and calls window.request_redraw() each frame.
- Keep the `mod ansi; mod audio; mod state_detector; mod tmux;` declarations so existing modules compile.

Note: code will compile but cannot display on screen in Docker (no Wayland compositor). Use `#[allow(dead_code)]` where needed.
Success: `cargo check -p thermal-conductor` passes with no errors.

title: Basic text rendering
notes: glyphon + cosmic-text to render 'THERMAL CONDUCTOR' text in the wgpu window. Verify font loading (Hack Nerd Font).
prompt:
Depends on task 1 (renderer.rs must exist with WgpuState).

Add text rendering to crates/thermal-conductor/src/renderer.rs:
1. Add a `TextRenderer` struct wrapping `glyphon::TextRenderer` + `glyphon::TextAtlas` + `glyphon::FontSystem`.
2. In `TextRenderer::new(device: &wgpu::Device, queue: &wgpu::Queue, format: wgpu::TextureFormat)`: create `FontSystem::new()`, `SwashCache::new()`, `TextAtlas::new(device, queue, format)`, `glyphon::TextRenderer::new(&mut atlas, device, wgpu::MultisampleState::default(), None)`.
3. Add a method `render_text(label: &str, x: f32, y: f32, size: f32, color: glyphon::Color)` that creates a `Buffer`, sets metrics, sets text, and prepares a `TextArea`.
4. Integrate into WgpuState: add a `text_renderer: TextRenderer` field, call it in `WgpuState::render()` to draw 'THERMAL CONDUCTOR' at top-left in ThermalPalette::TEXT (#c4b5fd).
Success: `cargo check -p thermal-conductor` passes.

title: tmux session management
notes: On startup: create tmux session 'thermal-conductor'. Spawn N panes. On shutdown: optionally kill or detach. Use std::process::Command to call tmux. ALREADY IMPLEMENTED in tmux.rs — needs a session manager wrapper that wires it to ConductorConfig.

title: Capture single tmux pane
notes: Poll 'tmux capture-pane -t %0 -p -e' to get pane content with ANSI escapes. Parse ANSI into styled text. Render in wgpu viewport.

title: Render terminal text with thermal colors
notes: Map ANSI colors to thermal palette. Render captured pane content as styled text in a wgpu viewport with proper monospace grid.
prompt:
Depends on tasks 2 and 4 (renderer.rs with TextRenderer, capture.rs with PaneCapture).

Add to crates/thermal-conductor/src/renderer.rs:
1. A function `ansi_color_to_rgba(color: &AnsiColor) -> glyphon::Color` that maps:
   - AnsiColor::Default → glyphon::Color::rgba from ThermalPalette::TEXT (#c4b5fd)
   - AnsiColor::Indexed(n) → use standard 16-color terminal palette; for n >= 16 use xterm-256 formula
   - AnsiColor::Rgb(r,g,b) → glyphon::Color::rgba(r,g,b,255)
2. A method `WgpuState::render_capture(&mut self, capture: &PaneCapture, viewport: Rect)` where `Rect` is `{x:f32, y:f32, w:f32, h:f32}`. Iterates `capture.lines`, builds a glyphon text area per line, calls text_renderer.render with correct offsets.
3. Define `pub struct Rect { pub x: f32, pub y: f32, pub w: f32, pub h: f32 }` in renderer.rs.
Import AnsiColor from `crate::ansi::AnsiColor`.
Success: `cargo check -p thermal-conductor` passes.

title: Pane grid layout engine
notes: Split window into N viewports. Draw thermal-colored borders (active=red, inactive=cold purple). Support grid/sidebar/stack layouts.

title: Multi-pane capture + render
notes: Capture all tmux panes in parallel. Render each in its viewport. Dirty tracking — only re-render panes with new content.

title: Input routing
notes: Keyboard input → 'tmux send-keys -t %N' to focused pane. Mouse click to switch focus. Scroll for scrollback via 'tmux capture-pane -S -N'.

title: Click-to-focus sidebar
notes: Click any pane to expand it in a sidebar view (70% width). Other panes become thumbnails on the side.
prompt:
Depends on task 8.

Extend crates/thermal-conductor/src/layout.rs:
1. Add `fn set_focused(&mut self, pane_idx: usize)` — updates self.focused_pane and switches layout to Layout::Sidebar when a non-focused pane is clicked, or back to Layout::Grid when the already-focused pane is clicked (toggle).
2. Verify `compute_rects()` Sidebar branch: focused pane gets `Rect { x:0.0, y:0.0, w: self.window_width*0.70, h: self.window_height }`. Remaining panes divide the right 30% vertically.
3. Add `fn pane_at(&self, x: f32, y: f32) -> Option<usize>` — iterates compute_rects() and returns the first index whose Rect contains (x, y). Used by InputHandler.

In input.rs, update handle() to call layout.pane_at(x, y) for mouse clicks and pass result to FocusPane action, then call layout.set_focused().
Success: `cargo check -p thermal-conductor` passes.

title: Agent state detection
notes: Pattern match pane output for state (prompt=idle, output=running, error=failed). Color pane borders accordingly. Watch Claude hook files with notify crate. PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED — state_detector.rs has StateDetector/DetectedState. Needs wiring to PaneInfo + border colors.

title: Audio notifications
notes: rodio + PipeWire. Play sounds on state transitions: idle→running (ascending), complete (chime), error (alert). Load WAV/OGG from assets/sounds/. PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED — audio.rs has AudioManager/SoundEvent but play_sound is only compiled with 'audio' feature. Needs state-transition wiring.

title: D-Bus service
notes: zbus org.thermal.Conductor interface. Methods: CreatePane, SendKeys, GetPaneContent, FocusPane, SetLayout. Signals: AgentStateChanged.

title: thermal-ctl CLI
notes: CLI wrapper around D-Bus API. 'thermal-ctl create', 'thermal-ctl send', 'thermal-ctl focus', 'thermal-ctl state'. Replaces tmux send-keys muscle memory. PARTIALLY IMPLEMENTED — thermal_ctl.rs has full CLI structure with clap but all commands print stubs.
prompt:
thermal_ctl.rs already has a full clap CLI with all subcommands. Currently all commands print stubs. Replace stubs with real tmux calls so the CLI is useful before D-Bus is ready.

Update crates/thermal-conductor/src/bin/thermal_ctl.rs:
1. Add `use thermal_conductor` — but since it's a bin in the same crate, use a path dep. Instead, call tmux CLI via std::process::Command directly.
2. Commands::List: run `tmux list-panes -t thermal-conductor -F '#{pane_id} #{pane_current_command} #{pane_active}'` and pretty-print with thermal labels (◉ for active, ○ for inactive).
3. Commands::Capture { pane, lines }: run `tmux capture-pane -t {pane} -p -e -S -{lines}` and print raw output.
4. Commands::Send { pane, keys }: run `tmux send-keys -t {pane} "{keys_str}"` then `tmux send-keys -t {pane} Enter`.
5. Commands::Kill { pane }: run `tmux kill-pane -t {pane}`.
6. Commands::State { pane }: capture pane and look for prompt patterns ($ / ❯ at end of last line) to print IDLE/RUNNING. Use a simple heuristic.
7. Create/Focus/Layout: keep as stubs with a note '(D-Bus pending)'.

All Command::new("tmux") calls must handle NotFound error gracefully.
Success: `cargo check -p thermal-conductor` passes.

title: Git diff awareness
notes: Watch repos for changes via notify crate. Surface changed files in a HUD overlay. Highlight panes that triggered changes.

title: Thermal HUD overlay
notes: Scanlines, reticle crosshair, status readouts. FPS counter. Agent count. System temp if available.

title: Phase 2: Direct PTY (replace tmux)
notes: Future: nix::pty::openpty, epoll multiplexing, alacritty_terminal Grid. Keep D-Bus API identical. No changes needed until Phase 2 begins — this is a future milestone.
